Source_Species.reason: Results and Experimental Section identify AtALMT9 as from Arabidopsis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Experimental Section states Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Columbia.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Experimental Section explicitly says ecotype Columbia.

Applied_Species.reason: The transgene was introduced into rice CBP plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice in the paper is Oryza sativa/Nipponbare background.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: CBP construct was introduced into slac1-1 receptor derived from Nipponbare; this matches 'Nipponbare; slac1-1 background'.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Experimental Section states AtALMT9 was amplified under the control of the OsActin1 promoter.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results describe AtALMT9 as the aluminium-activated malate transporter and Figure 2a calls it a vacuolar anion channel.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 1c and text explicitly place AtALMT9-GFP at the vacuolar membrane.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AtALMT9 is included as malate transporter module in pathway leading to malate storage.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Malate is an organic acid; this is consistent with paper context and metabolite analysis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Text states malate is transported to vacuoles and stored there; AtALMT9 localizes to vacuolar membrane.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The CAM model in Results describes nighttime malate storage in vacuoles and daytime mobilization supporting decarboxylation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Nighttime malate accumulation is shown for the whole multigene CBP system, but AtALMT9-specific contribution was not isolated experimentally.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Items 1 and 2 are supported, but item 3 infers AtALMT9's role from whole-pathway malate/pH data rather than direct gene-specific testing.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used in the study for AtALMT9 within CBP: stacking, localization, PCR/RT-PCR/immunoblot, metabolite and vacuolar pH assays.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Expression analyses at DNA/RNA/protein levels showed the six foreign genes, including AtALMT9, were expressed in CBP lines.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Malate and vacuolar pH were assessed across day/night, including 12:00 am and 12:00 pm sampling.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports AtALMT9 as part of a CAM engineering module, but the broader application statement is interpretive rather than directly tested.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper shows these tradeoffs in the full CBP context and does not isolate them to AtALMT9, matching the field wording.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Both clauses are supported: AtALMT9 was tested only in a multigene pathway, and Discussion notes daytime malate remobilization remains incomplete/needs further study.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice in this study is Oryza sativa.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: CRISPR construct was introduced into Nipponbare calli.

Applied_Species.reason: OsSLAC1 was edited and tested in rice.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Results mention slac1-1 and slac1-2 lines derived from Nipponbare.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: SLAC1 is described as part of the stomatal regulation module; Figure 2a refers to vacuolar anion channel for AtALMT9, not OsSLAC1, but SLAC1 anion channel identity is directly implied by gene name and cited prior work. Family term SLAH is not explicitly stated.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: OsSLAC1 is a stomatal regulator; the paper does not directly link OsSLAC1 itself to malate as a terminal metabolite.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Depends on unsupported assignment of malate as OsSLAC1 terminal metabolite.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: OsSLAC1 was not directly shown to control leaf vacuolar malate accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The wording carefully says it supports the CAM-like carbon sequestration context by stomatal behavior rather than direct malate synthesis, which matches the paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: slac1-1 was used as receptor background; CBP/slac1 context showed higher stomatal conductance and no improved WUE/drought resistance.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four points are directly supported by Results and Supporting Information references.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout, mutant phenotyping, drought treatment, and gas exchange were all used.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed conditions/treatments appear in Results: normal growth, PEG drought, pot drought, dark treatment, Ci and light variation.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly says constitutive stomatal opening is not suitable for drought-prone crops and suggests inducible/specific promoters for future stomatal regulation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: All listed tradeoffs are directly reported in Results/Discussion.

Other_Important_Info.reason: OsSLAC1 was used as receptor background/stomatal module rather than a metabolic catalyst; it is central to the synthetic CAM system.
